content: Prefers appointments after 2pm — mentioned childcare constraints during morning hours
Prefers to be addressed as 'Mrs Park' — corrected the agent when called by first name
Always requests Dr Chen specifically — declined alternative doctors when offered
Mum Sarah prefers text confirmation for bookings but wants a phone call for any changes or cancellations
Tends to cancel Friday appointments (4 of 5 Friday bookings were cancelled). Tuesday and Wednesday attendance is 100%
Cancels Monday appointments frequently (3 of 4 cancelled). Reliable on other weekdays
3 of 4 missed appointments were Wednesday mornings. Tuesday and Thursday attendance is 100%
Consistently books follow-up within 48 hours of appointment completion — proactive scheduler
Follows exact quarterly diabetes management cycle: fasting blood test → results review (1 week) → medication adjustment if needed
Needs extra appointment time — mobility issues mean she arrives slowly and needs help settling in
Becomes defensive about billing topics — responds better to empathetic, factual tone rather than formal statements
All communications should go through father Wei Zhang (0433 111 222) — never contact the patient directly
Daughter manages all bookings and appointment changes. Patient cannot use phone independently.
Does not respond to SMS or email — phone calls only. Confirmed in two separate conversations.
Gets very anxious when receiving phone calls — always use SMS. Has specifically requested no calls.
Asthma management follows strict 3-monthly cycle — disruption causes anxiety about health management
Rheumatoid arthritis cycle is 6-weekly not quarterly — tighter monitoring due to methotrexate
Son Keith drives her to appointments — only available before 1pm. Do not schedule afternoon slots.
Wife Doris accompanies to all appointments and translates medical information — always CC her on follow-up
Uni student — only available after 3pm on weekdays or Friday mornings (no classes). Prefers telehealth.
Has a pattern of no-shows for early morning appointments (9:00-9:30). Attends reliably for mid-morning and afternoon slots.
Improved attendance since switching to earlier time slots — previously missed 4 afternoon appointments
Currently 28 weeks pregnant — antenatal check-ups increasing in frequency. May need to switch to weekly visits soon.
Prefers first appointment of the day — morning sickness is less severe before 10am
Always arrives 15 minutes early. If appointment is running late, gets noticeably agitated.
Daughter Amira (0412 555 678) acts as interpreter — call her first to arrange complex appointment discussions
Hard of hearing — prefers SMS over phone calls despite being elderly. Phone calls require shouting.
Consistently reschedules first booking then keeps the rescheduled appointment. Account for this pattern.
Specifically requests Dr Nguyen for HRT management — uncomfortable discussing menopause with male doctors
Consistently pays invoices within 24 hours of receiving them — never required a payment chase
New patient transferred from Melbourne — still waiting on complete medical records from previous practice
Prefers not to discuss mental health in waiting room — appreciates direct-to-room when arriving
Cancellation rate increases during high work-stress periods — typically March and September (end of quarter)
Type 1 diabetes (paediatric) — quarterly cycle aligned with school terms to minimise missed classes
Dad Mark always accompanies — prefers afternoon appointments after school pickup at 3:15pm
Very shy with new people — mum Jenny mentioned he takes 10 minutes to warm up. Give him space.
Mum Kate requests same appointment slot each visit (Wednesday 10:30am) to maintain ADHD routine consistency
Speaks basic conversational English but needs Mandarin interpreter for anything medical. Use simple language for scheduling.
Tends to book annual review in January then reschedule to February — consider booking directly for February
Responds well to gentle, warm tone. Direct or clinical language about mental health increases her anxiety.
Has disputed 3 of last 5 invoices — always resolved after showing detailed breakdown. Send itemised invoices proactively.
Always brings her own cushion for the waiting room chair. Has been coming since 2015 — loyalty patient.
Works from home — flexible on appointment times but prefers mid-week (Tue-Thu)
Mum Riya handles all bookings. Dad Arjun works in construction — only available for evening calls after 5:30pm
COPD spirometry must be done in morning when respiratory effort is best — afternoon results are unreliable
Sensitive about fertility topics — avoids discussing in reception area. Use private message or phone for any fertility-related follow-up.
Prefers early morning appointments before 9:30am — drops kids at school then comes straight to clinic
No-show rate drops to zero when sent an SMS reminder 2 hours before appointment. Standard 24hr reminder insufficient.
Knee injury recovery — cleared for light exercise but not contact sport. Needs updated specialist letter for insurance.
Sister of Noah Mitchell (pat-036) — mum Sarah prefers booking both siblings back-to-back to minimise clinic visits
Always confirms appointments via return SMS within 5 minutes of receiving reminder. Very responsive.
Dad Sam only available mornings due to shift work — must book before 12pm for accompanied visits
Daughter Lily Chen interprets — call Lily on 0412 775 330, not Mrs Chen directly
Appointments must be after 1:30pm — daughter Lily finishes work at 1pm and needs to be present to translate
Wheelchair user — needs ground-floor wheelchair-accessible room
Never book Room 3 — upstairs with no lift access
Prefers morning appointments — drops kids at school then comes straight to clinic
